channel.title: briefUpdatesMainNLbusinessAndMarkets
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from briefUpdatesMainNLbusinessAndMarkets, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: As Gates’ Epstein connection unnerves staff, his foundation looks for a way forward
item.description: The billionaire’s abrupt withdrawal from India’s AI Summit follows renewed scrutiny over his relations with Jeffrey Epstein.

item.title: When Bitcoin prices turned against Michael Saylor, he quietly pivoted to risky financial gambit at Strategy
item.description: Saylor has been offseting the Bitcoin drag by reverting to different and dangerous scheme: Issuing tons of preferred stock.

item.title: 4 Pricing Mistakes Consulting Startups Should Avoid
item.description: Launching a new consulting business? Here’s how experienced founders say you should think about your fees.

item.title: How AI could kill the return to office
item.description: From Silicon Valley to Wall Street, many executives think that bringing employees back to the office is the secret to restoring productivity . But the...

item.title: Cuba is quickly nearing a point of no return as the U.S. weaponizes its Venezuelan oil supplies
item.description: A U.S. resolution in Cuba may not require full regime change, as the Cuban leadership rapidly runs out of options.

item.title: Our brains are wired to ignore information. Here are neuroscience-backed tips for communicating memorably
item.description: The human brain is engineered to ignore most of what it sees and hears, according to the neuroscientists I interviewed for the audio original Viral Vo...

item.title: Want to Effortlessly Build a Number of New Habits by Only Changing 1 Thing? Science Says Use the Spillover Effect
item.description: Research shows making one positive change effortlessly leads to others.

item.title: 5 simple tips to hit breakthrough ideas
item.description: Below, George Newman shares five key insights from his new book, How Great Ideas Happen: The Hidden Steps Behind Breakthrough Success . George is an a...

item.title: Troubled Nvidia ally Supermicro pledged to hire a new CFO ‘immediately.’ That was 14 months ago.
item.description: “No one wants this job,” Cowen Partners search firm president Shawn Cole said.

item.title: The CEO Mistake No Company Is Catching—Until It Blows Up
item.description: When a single unscripted remark can trigger boycotts, stock dips, and employee revolts, the real crisis isn’t what leaders say—it’s how they say it.

item.title: This Corner of the Stock Market Is Outperforming by Its Most in Decades
item.description: Consumer staples have destroyed consumer discretionary in 2026.

item.title: ‘Happier in my career than I’ve ever been’: Why more seniors are ‘unretiring’
item.description: Stacie Haller, a consultant for executives, recently had a meeting with a former business owner in his early 80s. He’d sold his business, started play...

item.title: How Toxic Is Your Workplace? If These 6 Things Happen Every Day, It’s Worse Than You Think
item.description: If these warning signs feel normal where you work, the damage is already happening.

item.title: United Airlines to reserve best perks for its cardholders. Is the card worth it?
Hollywood is freaking out over a viral AI video showing Brad Pitt and Tom Cruise fighting
item.description: At first glance, it could be the trailer for a new Hollywood blockbuster starring Brad Pitt and Tom Cruise. “This was a 2 line prompt in seedance 2,” ...

item.title: Former Prince Andrew was arrested. Bill Gates backed away from a speech. For these power players, the Epstein walls are closing in
item.description: Many cite the need to avoid further “distraction” as they step down.

item.title: The S&P 500 is undergoing a historic shift that could reshape the stock market
Dow closes nearly 270 points lower as stocks decline amid risk-off sentiment
The U.S. economy has grown faster than anyone predicted. Here’s why.
This is the sobering way most Americans plan to use their tax refunds this year
